name: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Running circular at Garbí
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Running circular al Garbí
region_intro: Jogging routes around Estivella are set within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, offering diverse terrain from valley floors to mountain slopes. The region features rugged rocks, rich plant life, and elevations ranging between 200 and 600 meters. Natural springs and viewpoints over the Mediterranean Sea characterize the landscape, providing varied running experiences. Best jogging routes around Estivella The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Running circular at Garbí, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and leads to the panoramic El Garbí viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Font de Barraix Spring – Castillo de Serra loop from Estivella, a difficult 11.7 miles (18.9 km) path. This route explores the area around the Font de Barraix spring and passes by the historic Castillo de Serra. Local runners also love the Font de Barraix Spring – Barraix Trail loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through varied natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours. Jogging around Estivella is defined by mountainous terrain, natural springs, and panoramic viewpoints, with options for challenging runs and routes through natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, though most routes are considered difficult. The routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Estivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Estivella?
A: Estivella offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 10 different trails available for exploration. These routes are set within the beautiful Sierra Calderona Natural Park, providing diverse landscapes for every jogger.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Estivella?
A: The jogging routes in Estivella feature diverse terrain, ranging from valley floors to mountain slopes. You'll encounter rugged rocks, rich plant life, and elevations between 200 and 600 meters. While most routes are considered difficult, they offer varied running experiences through natural parks and mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Estivella?
A: While many routes in Estivella are rated as difficult due to significant elevation gain, there is at least one moderate option available. For those seeking less strenuous options, "El Pla de Estivella" Leisure and Nature Park offers natural spaces that can be utilized for jogging.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in the Estivella area?
A: Yes, Estivella is known for its challenging running trails. Most of the routes are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain and varied terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31906425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Barraix Spring – Castillo de Serra loop from Estivella&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.7-mile path with over 800 meters of elevation gain, perfect for experienced runners.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while jogging near Estivella?
A: Jogging near Estivella offers stunning natural features and panoramic viewpoints. Many routes lead to the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views over the Mediterranean Sea and the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You can also discover natural springs like Font del Barraix, which is often included in circular routes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks along the running trails in Estivella?
A: Yes, you can blend physical activity with cultural discovery. Some routes may pass by historical elements such as the ruins of Beselga castle, Els Arcs aqueduct, or the Santos Juanes church. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31906425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Barraix Spring – Castillo de Serra loop from Estivella&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores the area around the Font de Barraix spring and passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Estivella?
A: The Sierra Calderona Natural Park, where Estivella is located, is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas with diverse flora and fauna, and to respect local regulations. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes available from Estivella?
A: Yes, Estivella offers several well-defined circular jogging routes. Popular options include those incorporating the Mirador del Garbí and Fuente de Barraix, or Fuente de Barraix and Senda de Barraix. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832291772/parque-natural-de-la-serra-calderona-running-circular-at-garbi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Running circular at Garbí&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a circular trail leading to a panoramic viewpoint.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Estivella?
A: Thanks to its proximity to the sea, Estivella enjoys a mild climate, making it an appealing destination for jogging year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential cooler, wetter days in winter.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Estivella?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Estivella is a small town, and parking is generally available within the municipality. Many routes originate directly from Estivella, making it convenient to park in or near the town center and access the trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Estivella?
A: The running routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Estivella's varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural beauty, challenging ascents, and panoramic views, especially from El Garbí.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views of the Mediterranean?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Estivella are designed to showcase the region's spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832291772/parque-natural-de-la-serra-calderona-running-circular-at-garbi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Running circular at Garbí&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading directly to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking panoramic vistas over the Mediterranean Sea and the surrounding natural park.
tips: Typical mountain of the region, the Garbí, has several different climbs and can be accessed by road, road, path or even climbing.
